data.title: The Vita Coco Company Set to Join S&P SmallCap 600
data.description: On March 20, 2026, the Vita Coco Company Inc. (NASD: COCO) announced it will join the S&P SmallCap 600 index, taking the place of TEGNA Inc. (NYSE: TGNA), starting on March 25. As part of the changes, Nexstar Media Group Inc. (NASD: NXST), a current member of the S&P MidCap 400, has acquired...

data.title: BYUtv Wins 2 Children's and Family Emmy® Awards from 14 Nominations
data.description: BYUtv in Provo, Utah announced on March 20, 2026, the nomination of several titles for awards. The nominated titles, which include "The Primrose Railway Children," "Secrets at Red Rocks," "Malory Towers," and "All-Round Champion," showcase the network's commitment to providing inspiring family entertainment. BYUtv, known for its free streaming and availability on cable and satellite platforms, has garnered two nominations as a recognition of their dedication to producing quality content.

It sounds like sci-fi… but it’s happening now. In China, researchers are turning thoughts into action with non-invasive brain-computer interface tech—already controlling real devices like wheelchairs.

data.title: PitchBook Earns Spot on G2's 2026 Best Financial Services Software Products List
data.description: A prominent private capital market intelligence provider, PitchBook, based in Seattle, has achieved recognition by being listed on G2’s 2026 Best Software Awards. Within the Best Financial Services Software Products category, PitchBook secured the sixth position, an advancement of two spots compared to the previous year's ranking. The prestigious acknowledgment is solely based on verified customer reviews, positioning PitchBook amongst a distinguished cohort of software vendors highly esteemed by their users. The annual G2 Best Software Awards highlight the top performers in various sectors, reflecting the trust and satisfaction of the customer base.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N -- Cyngn/
data.description: Cyngn has made significant progress in enhancing high-fidelity forklift simulation at GTC through FMU integration in NVIDIA Isaac Sim. The news was released on 16-Mar-2026 by Cyngn over PR Newswire. The revised release includes updates on the advancements made.

data.title: DELPHX ANNOUNCES CLOSING OF NON-BROKERED UNIT PRIVATE PLACEMENT
data.description: Toronto-based company DelphX Capital Markets Inc. (TSXV: DELX) (OTCQB: DPXCF) has successfully completed its non-brokered private placement, as per the announcement made on March 18, 2026. The placement involved the issuance of 4,360,000 units at a subscription price of C$0.05 per unit, generating total gross proceeds amounting to C$218,000. Each unit comprises one common share along with one common share purchase warrant. The warrant permits the holder to procure one common share at $0.08 within a two-year timeframe from the date of issuance.

data.title: Leading Brain Injury Experts Gather in Pittsburgh, Boost Groundbreaking Research at Chuck Noll Foundation's Scientific Symposium
data.description: Today in Pittsburgh, as the NFL Draft draws attention from football fans worldwide, and the Chuck Noll Foundation (CNF) for Brain Injury Research nears its 10-year milestone, leading experts in the industry convened for a significant event.

data.title: Federal judge declares Pentagon restrictions on press unconstitutional, upholding First Amendment in lawsuit by The New York Times. #PressFreedom
data.description: In a recent development, a federal judge has ruled against certain limitations imposed by the Pentagon on news outlets, stating that they were in violation of the First Amendment. The case was brought forward by The New York Times. This decision marks a significant win for the freedom of press, with the court intervening to protect this essential right. The ruling serves as a reminder of the importance of upholding the principles of the First Amendment.

data.title: Investor Alert: Berger Montague (Canada) PC represents investors that purchased shares of Super Micro Computer, Inc. listed on the CBOE Canada and elsewhere
data.description: A Toronto law firm called Berger Montague (Canada) PC, specializing in assisting investors in international shareholder disputes, has been appointed by an Ontario court to represent a Canadian-focused shareholder class action involving Super Micro Computer.

data.title: Tesla FSD Poised To Win European Approval. But TSLA Stock Completes A Round Trip.
data.description: Dutch regulators are expected to approve Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology by April 10. However, the electric vehicle company's stock experienced fluctuations after a surge towards the end of 2025. The news of Tesla's FSD potentially gaining approval in Europe was highlighted, along with the volatility in Tesla's stock price. Sources suggest that the regulatory approval for FSD could have implications for Tesla's market performance, emphasising the potential impact on investors.

data.title: TEAMSTERS ORGANIZE THIRD IKEA LOCATION IN NORTH AMERICA
data.description: In a significant organizing success in Joliet, Illinois, over 190 employees from IKEA's distribution center have decisively voted to become members of Teamsters Local 179. This achievement stands as the third victorious unionizing effort for the Teamsters.

data.title: Rhythm Grabs A New Obesity Approval; How It Edged In Where Lilly, Novo 'Have Fallen Flat'
data.description: On Friday, the stock of Rhythm Pharmaceuticals experienced a decline despite receiving FDA approval for its drug, Imcivree, to treat a genetic type of obesity. This approval sets them apart from competitors Lilly and Novo, who have not seen success in this area. The FDA's approval signifies a significant step for Rhythm Pharmaceuticals, even though their stock closed lower by the end of the day.

data.title: Judge rules Pentagon violated First Amendment by restricting press, favoring NY Times in lawsuit.
data.description: The First Amendment rights of news outlets were upheld by a federal judge in a recent ruling on a lawsuit filed by The New York Times against the Pentagon. The judge found that the Pentagon's limitations on news organizations were unconstitutional. This decision is significant for media freedom, as it highlights the importance of allowing journalists to report without undue restrictions. The ruling underscores the protection of free speech and press rights guaranteed by the Constitution.

data.title: Record number of DOs secure residency positions through NRMP Match
data.description: In Chicago on March 20, 2026, osteopathic medical students and graduates have attained unprecedented success in the 2026 National Resident Matching Program (NRMP) Match, setting a new record for the fourth year in a row with a rising number of residency positions secured for DOs. This achievement highlights the continued growth and advancement in the field.

data.title: Guardian Capital Group Limited Announces Expected Closing Date For Take-Private Transaction With Desjardins
data.description: Guardian Capital Group Limited has announced that it has received regulatory approval to finalize a statutory plan of arrangement under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario). Desjardins Global Asset Management Inc., a Desjardins Group affiliate, will acquire all Common shares and Class A shares of Guardian, with the exception of shares held by specific shareholders with equity rollover agreements, at a price of C$68.00 per share in cash. The deal marks a significant development for Guardian and its shareholders.

data.title: HYTN Appoints Fabian Monaco to Board of Directors
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 20, 2026, HYTN Innovations Inc. (CSE: HYTN, FSE: 85W0, OTC PINK: HYTNF) disclosed the appointment of Mr. Fabian Monaco to its Board of Directors. As a pharmaceutical manufacturer focusing on cannabis, HYTN welcomed Mr. Monaco to the board effective immediately.
